Entity_Description: Cortes Island is an unincorporated community in the Discovery Islands of British Columbia, within the Strathcona Regional District off the northeast coast of Vancouver Island. As of the 2021 Census it had a population of 1,050. Reached by two ferries from Campbell River via Quadra Island, the island supports a shellfish-aquaculture industry, notably oyster and clam farming, alongside tourism, arts and small-scale forestry. Small settlements include Whaletown, Manson's Landing, Squirrel Cove and Cortes Bay, and it is home to the Klahoose First Nation and the Hollyhock retreat centre.

Long_Description:
Cortes Island sits at the outer edge of the Discovery Islands, and getting here tells you most of what a {BUSINESS_TYPE} needs to know: you drive to Campbell River, ferry to Quadra Island, cross Quadra, then catch a second ferry into Whaletown. That two-ferry reality shapes every {SERVICE} job on the island, from scheduling materials to timing crews around the last sailing.

The community of roughly 1,050 is scattered across small settlements: Whaletown in the northwest, Squirrel Cove and Cortes Bay to the east, and Manson's Landing and Smelt Bay along the sheltered southern shore. Many homes here are waterfront cabins, off-grid acreages, and seasonal retreats rather than subdivision tract housing, so {COMPANY_NAME} plans {SERVICE} around well water, solar and propane systems, and long gravel driveways that never appear on a template quote.

The island's economy leans on the sea and the forest. Shellfish aquaculture, with oyster and clam farming in Gorge Harbour, Cortes Bay and the surrounding inlets, sits alongside tourism, arts and small-scale forestry, and the Klahoose First Nation is central to the community. Visitors arrive for the Hollyhock retreat centre near Manson's Landing, for swimming at freshwater Hague Lake, and for the beaches of Smelt Bay and Manson's Landing Provincial Parks. Each of these draws a different kind of property owner with different {SERVICE_CATEGORY} needs.

Then there's the weather. Cortes shares the wet coastal temperate rainforest climate of the central island: mild, damp winters and green shoulder seasons tempered by the Pacific, with abundant rainfall that tests roofs, drainage, decks and exteriors year-round. A {SERVICE} provider who ignores that moisture, or who underestimates how island humidity and salt air work on waterfront structures, leaves clients with problems by the next storm season.

{COMPANY_NAME} approaches Cortes Island the way locals do: patiently, and with respect for the logistics. We coordinate ferry timing, bundle work to reduce trips, and understand the difference between a Cortes Bay waterfront build and an off-grid cabin up toward Whaletown. From routine {SERVICE} to larger projects, our {SERVICE_LIST} is delivered with the reliability island owners rarely find from off-island crews. {UNIQUE_VALUE_PROP} For {SERVICE} that genuinely understands Cortes Island, call {COMPANY_NAME} at {PHONE}.
Local_Facts:
- Population: 1,050 (2021 Census)
- Region: Vancouver Island Central & North; Strathcona Regional District
- One of the Discovery Islands, reached by two ferries from Campbell River via Quadra Island (Heriot Bay to Whaletown)
- Home to the Klahoose First Nation
- Shellfish aquaculture, including oyster and clam farming, is a key local industry alongside tourism and forestry
- The Hollyhock retreat centre near Manson's Landing is a noted educational and wellness destination
- Provincial parks include Smelt Bay and Manson's Landing, the latter near freshwater Hague Lake
- Climate: mild, wet coastal temperate rainforest tempered by the Pacific with abundant rainfall
